steps.title: Rule out impossible tables
steps.content: Choices A and B each list two different $y$-values for $x=0$, which is impossible for a function. One input cannot produce two outputs. This eliminates A and B, leaving C and D to test.

steps.title: Match the vertex to the correct table
steps.content:
The parabola's minimum point is located at $x=3$, $y=-12$.
  • Choice C includes the point $(3, -12)$
  • Choice D has no point at $x=3$
The three points in choice C—$(0, 0)$, $(3, -12)$, $(6, 0)$—represent the start, vertex, and end of the parabola. That matches choice C — C is correct.
